slides.title: Fun and Games
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 3: Fun and Games. In this unit, your child learns the words for games and play — action words like bounce, roll, throw and catch. They learn the names of parts of the body, and words that say where things are (on, under, next to), and left and right. They play simple games, learn a game from another country, and listen to a funny story. Along the way they hear the short 'u' sound (sun, fun, duck). The goal is happy, active English — learning through play. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and act out ball actions (bounce, roll, throw, catch, hit)
Name parts of the body (hand, foot, ear, nose, shoulder, head…)
Say where something is (on, under, next to) and show left and right
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and act out ball actions (bounce, roll, throw, catch, hit). you will also name parts of the body (hand, foot, ear, nose, shoulder, head…). you will also say where something is (on, under, next to) and show left and right. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: bounce, roll, throw, catch, hit, run. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Amal and the Big Ball and Talk about Fun and Games. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can ___.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can ___. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
